revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天津神社 (習志野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| |name = Amatsu Shrine (天津神社) |image = File:IMG yaturugi.JPG |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Narashino|en|Narashino|qid=Q318047|lt=Narashino City|1=Narashino}}{{ill|Yashiki|de|Yashiki|ja|屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiki|1=Yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q116479050}}3-12 |ISO = JP-12 |latitude degrees = 35|latitude minutes = 40|latitude seconds = 53 |longitude degrees = 140|longitude minutes = 03|longitude seconds = 25 |label position = right |map = Japan Chiba#Japan |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Old Shrine Rank|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} |shrine Rank = Ungraded Shrine |foundation = Unknown |main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Ryuzo|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |alias = |sacred objects = |festival = September 22 |ritual = |location = {{ill|Narashino|qid=Q318047|1=Narashino}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.68117222222222|N|140.0568888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Amatsu Shrine''' (天津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Narashino|en|Narashino|qid=Q318047|lt=Narashino City|1=Narashino}} area of {{ill|Yashiki|de|Yashiki|ja|屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiki|1=Yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q116479050}}. Its former shrine Rank according to the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Old Shrine Rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is Ungraded Shrine.<ref name="amatsu"/> ==Enshrined Deity== *'''{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}''' ==History== It is said to have been founded by the {{ill|Chiba clan|en|Chiba clan|qid=Q2975237|lt=Chiba Clan|1=Chiba clan}} branch known as the {{ill|Maka Clan|de|Maka-Klan|ja|馬加氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maka Clan|1=Maka Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667824}}. According to legend, {{ill|Maka Castle|de|Maka-Burg|ja|馬加城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maka Castle|1=Makuwari Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667822}} (present-day {{ill|Chiba (city)|en|Chiba (city)|qid=Q170616|lt=Chiba City|1=Chiba (city)}}{{ill|Hanamigawa-ku|en|Hanamigawa-ku|qid=Q1075882|lt=Hanamigawa Ward|1=Hanamigawa-ku}} Makuhari Town) was the seat of {{ill|Maka Yasutomi|de|Maka Yasutomi|ja|馬加康胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maka Yasutomi|1=Makuwari Yasutane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667823}}, who, during the reconstruction of his castle, relocated to this area and built a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrining the guardian deity of the Chiba Clan, {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|qid=Q10960623|lt=Myoken Bosatsu|1=Myōken}}.<ref name="amatsu">{{Cite web|type=Japanese book|url=http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=%5E$&search:word=1123|title=Amatsu Shrine|website=Shrine Forum|access-date=2020-03-15|qq=}}</ref><ref> {{Cite sign |title=Shrine Structure Commentary Version |quote=In the 4th year of Heisei (1992), on an auspicious day in January. The exact date of the deity’s installation is not detailed, but according to an ancient document of the shrine, it is recounted that in the ancient Makuhari village a descendant of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}}’s 50th generation – an imperial prince from the {{ill|Shikibu-shō|en|Shikibu-shō|qid=Q3727885|lt=District of the Ministry of Ceremonial|1=Shikibu-shō}} (as per the original text) – namely, the later descendant {{ill|Prince Katsuhara|de|Prinz Katsuhara|ja|葛原親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Katsuhara|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620877}}, the son of {{ill|Chiba Tsune|de|Chiba Tsune|ja|千葉常胤|lt=Chiba Kaihei Tsune|lt_ja=千葉介平常胤|lt_de=Chiba Kaihei Tsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsune|1=Chiba Tsunetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11406057}}, known as {{ill|Yashinobu|de|Yashinobu|ja|大須賀胤信|lt=Lord Yashinobu|lt_ja=胤信公|lt_de=Yashinobu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashinobu|1=Ōsuga Tanenobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11441884}}, became the castle lord; and in the era of {{ill|Maka Yasutomi|de|Maka Yasutomi|ja|馬加康胤|lt=Hira Yasutomi|lt_ja=平康胤|lt_de=Maka Yasutomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maka Yasutomi|1=Makuwari Yasutane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667823}}, his descendants, who were the castle lords of Makuhari, began residing in the castle under construction on Makuhari Platform (known since ancient times as Yashiki Platform, now Yashiki Town), and thereafter this area was called Makaya-shiki. Hira Yasutomi, in deep reverence for the deity, established the Amatsu Shrine in the Yashiki district and enshrined the Great Deity {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}.|qq=}}</ref> It is also said that the place name “Yashiki” derives from the residence where Yasutomi once lived.<ref>{{Cite web|type=Japanese book|url=https://www.city.narashino.lg.jp/joho/shokai/yurai.html|title=Origin of the Name|website=Narashino City Official Website|access-date=2020-03-15|qq=}}</ref> In the early Meiji period, due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri (Separation of Shinto and Buddhism)|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, it was renamed “Amatsu Shrine” and the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}. ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|qid=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine|1=Sengen shrine}} ==Festivals== * '''Annual Festival''' (September 22) *: Every third year, a {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|qid=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} {{ill|Procession|en|Procession|qid=Q115665045|lt=Procession|1=Procession}} is held. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|Amatsu Shrine|de|Amatsu-Schrein|ja|天津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Shrine|1=Amatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16276796}} – Disambiguation page * {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|qid=Q10960623|lt=Myoken Bosatsu|1=Myōken}} * {{ill|Chiba Shrine|en|Chiba Shrine|qid=Q11406700|lt=Chiba Shrine|1=Chiba Shrine}} * {{ill|Maka Castle|de|Maka-Burg|ja|馬加城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maka Castle|1=Makuwari Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667822}} * {{ill|Maka Clan|de|Maka-Klan|ja|馬加氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maka Clan|1=Maka Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667824}} ==External Links== * [http://www.etown-okubo.net/map/map_jinja_tenshin.html Amatsu Shrine (Okubo Internet Shopping District)]
{{translated page|ja|天津神社 (習志野市)|version=97985534|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:天津神社 (習志野市)]] [[de:Amatsu-Schrein]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11442801}} [[Category:Chiba clan]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:習志野市の建築物]] [[Category:習志野市の歴史]]
